message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Fix StoryBrowser Layout in center-bar-holder-container

## Context
The StoryBrowser component doesn't fill the `.center-bar-holder-container` properly, and the layout shifts/looks inconsistent when switching between filter tabs (All, Ongoing, Completed, Hiatus). The root cause is that `.story-browser` lacks `width: 100%`, so it shrink-wraps to its content. Combined with the parent's `align-items: center`, this means different tab results produce different actual widths.

## Root Cause
- `.center-bar-holder-container` is a flex column with `align-items: center` and fixed widths (750px desktop, 550px tablet, 100% mobile)
- `.story-browser` only has `max-width: 750px` but **no explicit `width: 100%`** — so it collapses to content width
- The grid uses `auto-fill` which produces inconsistent column counts when the container width varies between tabs

## File to Modify
- `client/src/components/Stories/StoryBrowser/StoryBrowser.css`

## Changes

### 1. Fix `.story-browser` to fill container
Add `width: 100%` and `box-sizing: border-box` so it always takes the full center-bar width regardless of content.

### 2. Lock grid to 3 columns on desktop
Change `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr))` to `repeat(3, 1fr)` so the grid is always consistent no matter how many items are displayed. This prevents layout shifts between tabs.

### 3. Match existing app design conventions
Style the header, filters, and cards to use the same CSS variables and patterns as the rest of the app (font-family: `var(--font-ui)`, border colors from `var(--border-card)`, backgrounds from `var(--bg-card)`, etc.) — keeping it visually consistent with PostCards and other pages in the center bar.

### 4. Responsive grid
- Desktop (750px container): 3 columns
- Tablet (550px container): 2 columns
- Mobile (480px): 2 columns (already handled, just confirm `width: 100%`)

## Verification
1. Navigate to `/home/stories` — content fills the center bar width
2. Click All / Ongoing / Completed / Hiatus — layout stays the same width, no shifting
3. Check on tablet (768px) and mobile (480px) — consistent 2-column grid
4. Cards, filters, buttons match the app's visual style

Story Card Click Handler (StoryBrowser.jsx) **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Stories\StoryBrowser\StoryBrowser.jsx` (lines 137-143) The story card click handler is in the `StoryCard` component: ```jsx navigate(`/home/stories/${story.id}`)} /> ``` **Key Details:** - Navigates to `/home/stories/${story.id}` (the story detail page) - **No state is passed** - no previous filter tab info is retained - Simple navigation with just the storyId as a route parameter --- ### 2. Complete Story Routes (App.jsx) **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\App.jsx` (lines 133-141) ```jsx {/* Stories routes */} }/> }/> }/> }/> }/> }/> }/> }/> ``` All routes are nested under `/home`, so full paths are: - `/home/stories` - StoryBrowser (list of stories) - `/home/stories/dashboard` - StoryDashboard (user's own stories) - `/home/stories/new` - StoryEditor (create new story) - `/home/stories/:storyId` - **StoryDetail** (story detail page - DESTINATION) - `/home/stories/:storyId/edit` - StoryEditor (edit story details) - `/home/stories/:storyId/manage` - StoryChapterManager (manage chapters) - `/home/stories/:storyId/chapter/:chapterId` - ChapterReader (read chapter) - `/home/stories/:storyId/chapter/:chapterId/edit` - ChapterEditor (edit chapter) --- ### 3. Destination Component: StoryDetail.jsx **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Stories\StoryDetail\StoryDetail.jsx` #### Header/Hero Section Structure (lines 68-118): The StoryDetail page has a **hero section** with: ```jsx {/* Hero section */}
{story.cover_url ? : }

{story.title}

{author.image_url && } {author.name}
{status} {chapters.length} chapter(s) {totalWords} words {read_count} reads {vote_count} votes
{story.tags &&
...
}
``` #### Header CSS (StoryDetail.css): ```css .story-detail-hero { display: flex; gap: 20px; margin-bottom: 20px; } .story-detail-title { font-size: 1.4rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text-primary); line-height: 1.25; } .story-detail-author { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; cursor: pointer; } ``` #### Action Buttons (lines 121-145): Three main buttons below the hero: 1. **"Start Reading" or "Continue Reading"** - Navigates to first chapter or saved reading position 2. **Vote button** - Shows vote count (heart icon) 3. **Library button** - "Add to Library" or "In Library" toggle #### Content Sections Below: - **About section** - Story description - **Chapters list** - Clickable list of all chapters, each navigates to `/home/stories/${storyId}/chapter/${ch.id}` --- ### 4. Chapter Reader Destination **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Stories\ChapterReader\ChapterReader.jsx` When a chapter is clicked, it navigates to `/home/stories/${storyId}/chapter/${chapterId}`. #### Header/Heading Area (lines 162-176): ```jsx
Chapter {chapter.chapter_number}

{chapter.title}

``` #### Header CSS (ChapterReader.css): ```css .chapter-reader-header { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; padding: 20px 0 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-light); } .chapter-reader-back { width: 36px; height: 36px; background: var(--bg-secondary); border-radius: 8px; flex-shrink: 0; } .chapter-reader-title { font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text-primary); } ``` #### Back Navigation: - **Back button** (line 166) navigates to `/home/stories/${storyId}` (back to StoryDetail) - **Previous/Next chapter buttons** at bottom (lines 203-225) navigate between chapters within the same story --- ### 5. Back Navigation Pattern in App The app uses a simple **window.history.back()** pattern for some components (as seen in ContentView.jsx line 258): ```jsx const handleBackLocation = (e) => { e.stopPropagation(); window.history.back(); } ``` However, **StoryDetail and ChapterReader use explicit route-based navigation** (more reliable): - ChapterReader: `navigate(\`/home/stories/${storyId}\`)` - StoryDetail: No back button visible - Both use explicit route URLs instead of history.back() --- ### 6. Complete Navigation Flow Summary ``` StoryBrowser (/home/stories) ↓ [click story card] StoryDetail (/home/stories/:storyId) ├─ [click chapter] → ChapterReader (/home/stories/:storyId/chapter/:chapterId) │ ├─ [back button] → StoryDetail │ ├─ [prev chapter] → ChapterReader (prev) │ └─ [next chapter] → ChapterReader (next) │ └─ [start reading] → ChapterReader (first or saved chapter) StoryDashboard (/home/stories/dashboard) [user's own stories] ├─ [click card] → StoryChapterManager (/home/stories/:storyId/manage) │ └─ [back button] → StoryDashboard │ └─ [edit button] → StoryEditor (/home/stories/:storyId/edit) StoryBrowser also has: ├─ "My Stories" button → StoryDashboard └─ "+ Write" button → StoryEditor (/home/stories/new) ``` --- ### 7. Key Architectural Patterns 1. **No state preservation** - Clicking a story card from StoryBrowser doesn't pass filter state (like which status tab was active) 2. **Independent LexicalComposer instances** - ChapterReader wraps its content in its own `` (line 180) 3. **Route-based navigation** - All story navigation uses `navigate(path)` rather than `window.history.back()` 4. **Reading progress tracking** - StoryDetail checks `story.reading_progress.chapter_id` to offer "Continue Reading" instead of "Start Reading" 5. **Consistent hero layout** - Both StoryDetail and ChapterReader have similar header structures (cover/info for StoryDetail, chapter label/title for ChapterReader)

# Add Back Button to StoryDetail + Preserve Filter State

## Context
When you click a story card in StoryBrowser, you land on StoryDetail with no way to go back. The user wants a back button in the StoryDetail header that returns them to the exact filter tab (All, Ongoing, Completed, Hiatus) they came from.

Currently:
- StoryBrowser stores the filter in local `useState` — it resets on re-mount
- Story card click does `navigate(/home/stories/${story.id})` with no state passed
- StoryDetail has no back button at all

## Approach

### 1. StoryBrowser — persist filter in URL search params
**File:** `client/src/components/Stories/StoryBrowser/StoryBrowser.jsx`

- Import `useSearchParams` from react-router-dom
- Replace `useState(null)` for `statusFilter` with `searchParams.get('status')`
- When a filter tab is clicked, update the search param (`?status=ongoing`) instead of local state
- This means when the browser navigates back, the URL still has `?status=ongoing` and the correct tab is restored

### 2. StoryDetail — add back button to header
**File:** `client/src/components/Stories/StoryDetail/StoryDetail.jsx`

- Add a back button above the hero section, following the same pattern as ChapterReader's `.chapter-reader-back` button
- Use `navigate(-1)` (browser back) — this naturally returns to `/home/stories?status=ongoing` preserving the filter
- Add a header row with the back arrow + "Stories" label (like ChapterReader has chapter label + title)

### 3. StoryDetail CSS — style the back button
**File:** `client/src/components/Stories/StoryDetail/StoryDetail.css`

- Add `.story-detail-header` (flex row with gap, border-bottom, same as `.chapter-reader-header`)
- Add `.story-detail-back` button (36x36, `var(--bg-secondary)`, rounded 8px — identical to `.chapter-reader-back`)
- Add `.story-detail-header-label` for the "Stories" text

## Files to Modify
1. `client/src/components/Stories/StoryBrowser/StoryBrowser.jsx` — switch filter from useState to useSearchParams
2. `client/src/components/Stories/StoryDetail/StoryDetail.jsx` — add back button header
3. `client/src/components/Stories/StoryDetail/StoryDetail.css` — style the back button

## Verification
1. Go to `/home/stories` → click "Ongoing" tab → click a story → see back button at top
2. Click back button → returns to `/home/stories?status=ongoing` with Ongoing tab active
3. Repeat for Completed, Hiatus, All tabs — each preserves correctly
4. Back button style matches ChapterReader's back button exactly

# Fix StoryBrowser Layout in center-bar-holder-container

## Context
The StoryBrowser component doesn't fill the `.center-bar-holder-container` properly, and the layout shifts/looks inconsistent when switching between filter tabs (All, Ongoing, Completed, Hiatus). The root cause is that `.story-browser` lacks `width: 100%`, so it shrink-wraps to its content. Combined with the parent's `align-items: center`, this means different tab results produce different actual widths.

## Root Cause
- `.center-bar-holder-container` is a flex column with `align-items: center` and fixed widths (750px desktop, 550px tablet, 100% mobile)
- `.story-browser` only has `max-width: 750px` but **no explicit `width: 100%`** — so it collapses to content width
- The grid uses `auto-fill` which produces inconsistent column counts when the container width varies between tabs

## File to Modify
- `client/src/components/Stories/StoryBrowser/StoryBrowser.css`

## Changes

### 1. Fix `.story-browser` to fill container
Add `width: 100%` and `box-sizing: border-box` so it always takes the full center-bar width regardless of content.

### 2. Lock grid to 3 columns on desktop
Change `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr))` to `repeat(3, 1fr)` so the grid is always consistent no matter how many items are displayed. This prevents layout shifts between tabs.

### 3. Match existing app design conventions
Style the header, filters, and cards to use the same CSS variables and patterns as the rest of the app (font-family: `var(--font-ui)`, border colors from `var(--border-card)`, backgrounds from `var(--bg-card)`, etc.) — keeping it visually consistent with PostCards and other pages in the center bar.

### 4. Responsive grid
- Desktop (750px container): 3 columns
- Tablet (550px container): 2 columns
- Mobile (480px): 2 columns (already handled, just confirm `width: 100%`)

## Verification
1. Navigate to `/home/stories` — content fills the center bar width
2. Click All / Ongoing / Completed / Hiatus — layout stays the same width, no shifting
3. Check on tablet (768px) and mobile (480px) — consistent 2-column grid
4. Cards, filters, buttons match the app's visual style
